question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: nodeMapDecodeHookFunc()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML config file via HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG where the nodes map contains a key with an invalid IP address format, causing the SDK client to be initialized with an unreachable endpoint and preventing transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: nodeMapDecodeHookFunc()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML config file via HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG where the nodes map contains a key with port 0 (e.g., '10.0.0.1:0'), causing the SDK client to attempt connections on an invalid port and preventing all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: nodeMapDecodeHookFunc()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML config file via HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG where the nodes map contains a key with a hostname instead of an IP address that resolves to an attacker-controlled server, causing all gossiped transactions to be intercepted?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: nodeMapDecodeHookFunc()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML config file via HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG where the nodes map contains a key with an account ID of '0.0.0' (zero account), causing the SDK client to route transactions to an invalid node account and preventing successful gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: nodeMapDecodeHookFunc()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML config file via HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG where the nodes map contains only one entry pointing to a non-consensus node account ID (e.g., '0.0.1'), causing the SDK client to attempt gossip to a non-node entity and failing all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user place a malicious application.yml file in the current working directory of the Rosetta process (e.g., via a shared filesystem or container volume mount), causing v.AddConfigPath('.') to load it and override node configuration with attacker-controlled values that redirect gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user place a malicious application.yml in the current working directory that sets hiero.mirror.rosetta.online to false, causing the server to start in offline mode and disabling all transaction gossip without any authentication check?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user place a malicious application.yml in the current working directory that sets hiero.mirror.rosetta.nodes to a map of attacker-controlled endpoints, overriding the default network nodes and redirecting all transaction gossip to the attacker's infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user place a malicious application.yml in the current working directory that sets hiero.mirror.rosetta.network to 'mainnet' when the service is deployed for testnet, causing all transactions to be gossiped to the wrong network and effectively preventing them from reaching their intended destination?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user place a malicious application.yml in the current working directory that sets hiero.mirror.rosetta.nodeRefreshInterval to '1ms', causing the SDK client to refresh the node address book so frequently that it creates excessive network traffic and degrades performance, preventing timely transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user place a malicious application.yml in the current working directory that sets hiero.mirror.rosetta.db.pool.maxOpenConnections to 1, creating a severe database connection bottleneck that prevents the construction service from processing multiple concurrent transaction gossip requests?
